paper.title: On the origins of extreme velocity stars as revealed by large-scale Galactic surveys

extraction.summary: Extracted 52 HVS candidates from Table 2. 40 are new discoveries by this paper, 12 were previously reported by Bromley2018 and Li2021.

method_chain.summary: Adopt spectroscopic data from RAVE DR5, SDSS DR12, LAMOST DR8, APOGEE DR16, GALAH DR2, and astrometric data from Gaia EDR3.

source_refs.context: Data section describing the surveys used

method_chain.summary: Cross-match spectroscopic survey targets to Gaia EDR3 using TOPCAT with 3 arcsec matching radius.
outputs: cross-matched catalog with astrometry and spectroscopy

source_refs.context: Cross-matching spectroscopic surveys to Gaia EDR3

method_chain.summary: Apply quality cuts: spectral S/N > 10, vlos uncertainty < 30 km/s, parallax >= 0.2 mas, sigma_pi/pi <= 20%, RUWE < 1.4, visual inspection of bad spectra, and exclusion of variable stars and white dwarfs.

source_refs.context: HiVel star selection criteria and quality filters

method_chain.summary: Measure and correct radial velocities from spectroscopic surveys using radial velocity standard stars. Apply zero-point offsets: -7.3 km/s for SDSS.

source_refs.context: Radial velocity zero-point correction using standard stars

method_chain.summary: Estimate Bayesian distances from Gaia EDR3 parallaxes with zero-point correction and thin/thick disk/halo density prior. Update prior based on disk/halo classification.

source_refs.context: Bayesian distance estimation from parallax

method_chain.summary: Calculate 3D velocities in Cartesian and Galactocentric cylindrical systems, and derive total velocity V_GSR. Propagate uncertainties.

source_refs.context: Velocity calculation from distances, proper motions, and radial velocities

method_chain.summary: Select HiVel sample with V_GSR >= 300 km/s, classify into disk and halo populations, iterate distance and velocity estimates twice until convergence. Final sample: 12,784 HiVels (6966 halo, 5818 disk).

source_refs.context: Sample selection and disk/halo classification process

method_chain.summary: Adopt five Galactic potential models to estimate escape velocities: MilkyWayPotential (Gala), Monari et al. 2018, Deason et al. 2019, BovyMWPotential2014 (Gala), and Williams et al. 2017 measured curve.

source_refs.context: Adoption of four Galactic potential models and one measured escape velocity curve

method_chain.summary: Perform 2000 Monte Carlo simulations per star sampling measurement errors to estimate V_GSR distributions. Calculate unbound probability P_ub as the frequency of V_GSR exceeding the escape velocity curve.
outputs: unbound probabilities P_ub for five models

source_refs.context: Monte Carlo simulation for unbound probability calculation

method_chain.summary: Classify 52 HVS candidates with at least one group of P_ub >= 50% from the five models. 40 are new discoveries, 12 reported by Bromley2018 and Li2021.

source_refs.context: Classification of 52 HVS candidates with P_ub >= 50%

method_chain.summary: Reconstruct backward-integrated trajectories for 547 extreme velocity stars with V_GSR >= 0.8 V_esc in the Galactic potential.

source_refs.context: Backward-integrated trajectories for extreme velocity stars

method_chain.summary: Assess origins by analyzing closest approaches to Galactic center and subsystems (Sgr dSph, globular clusters, dwarf galaxies). No candidates definitely ejected from GC; 8 stars pass within 1 kpc of GC; 15 stars (including 2 HVS candidates) have close encounters with Sgr dSph.

source_refs.context: Origin assessment from orbital analysis

inclusion_assessment.summary: LG-HVS1 is one of 52 marginally hypervelocity star candidates presented by this paper with V_GSR exceeding the local escape velocity within 2-sigma confidence levels.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly labels these objects as HVS candidates in Table 2 and discusses their marginal unbound status with Monte Carlo unbound probabilities.

source_refs.context: Selection of 52 HVS candidates with P_ub >= 50% from five Galactic potential models
Table 2 caption: The 52 HVS candidates assuming different escape velocity curves
Selection of 52 HVS candidates with P_ub >= 50% from five Galactic potential models

hypothesis_metrics.hypothesis: unbound under Williams2017 observed escape velocity

Paper references Li2021 HiVel candidates

hypothesis_metrics.hypothesis: unbound under Williams2017 observed escape velocity

candidate_groups_considered.description: 52 HVS candidates from Table 2 with astrometric parameters, kinematics, and unbound probabilities under five Galactic potential models.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Paper explicitly presents these 52 stars as marginally hypervelocity star candidates that have V_GSR exceeding their local escape velocities within 2-sigma confidence levels, with at least one P_ub >= 50%.

source_refs.context: Selection of 52 HVS candidates with P_ub >= 50% from five Galactic potential models
Table 2 caption: The 52 HVS candidates assuming different escape velocity curves

candidate_groups_considered.description: 8 extreme velocity stars with backward-integrated orbits passing within 1 kpc of the Galactic center.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Paper presents these as extreme velocity stars with possible GC origin, not as HVS/unbound candidates. The paper states 'no candidates are found to be definitely ejected from the Galactic-center (GC), while eight metal-poor extreme velocity stars are found to have a closest distance to the GC within 1 kpc.' They are part of the 547 extreme velocity stars with V_GSR >= 0.8 V_esc, not the 52 HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: Figure 7 caption and orbital analysis: no candidates definitely ejected from GC, 8 stars within 1 kpc

candidate_groups_considered.description: 15 extreme velocity stars with close encounters with the Sagittarius dSph galaxy.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Two of these stars (SG-HVS43 and SG-HVS47) are HVS candidates already included from Table 2. The remaining 13 are extreme velocity stars with possible Sgr dSph origin but are not presented as unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Figure 8 caption describing 15 extreme velocity stars with Sgr dSph encounters, including 2 HVS candidates
